content.problemStatement:
In a season, each player has three statistics: runs, wickets, and catches. Given the season stats of two players AAA and BBB, denoted by RRR, WWW, and CCC respectively, the person who is better than the other in the most statistics is regarded as the better overall player. Tell who is better amongst AAA and BBB. It is known that in each statistic, the players have different values.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line contains three integers R1R_1R1​, W1W_1W1​, C1C_1C1​, the stats for player AAA.
The second line contains three integers R2R_2R2​, W2W_2W2​, C2C_2C2​, the stats for player BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line `"A"` (without quotes) if player AAA is better than player BBB and `"B"` (without quotes) otherwise.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains three integers R1 W1 C1 – the runs, wickets and catches of player A. The second line contains three integers R2 W2 C2 – the corresponding statistics of player B. All six numbers are distinct pairwise for each statistic (R1≠R2, W1≠W2, C1≠C2).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single character on its own line: "A" if player A is better in at least two of the three statistics, otherwise "B".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: B is better in all three fields.\nTest case 2: A is better in all three fields.\nTest case 3: A wins runs (10>0) and catches (10>0) while B wins wickets, so A wins 2-1.
A beats B in runs (5>2) and catches (3>0); B beats A only in wickets. Hence A is better.
First case: B wins runs and catches, A wins only wickets → B.\nSecond case: A wins all three statistics → A.

solution.approach: For each test case compare the three corresponding statistics. Keep two counters: aWin for the number of fields where A's value is larger, and bWin for B's. After processing the three fields, if aWin > bWin (which means A won at least two fields) print "A", otherwise print "B". The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        R1 = int(next(it)); W1 = int(next(it)); C1 = int(next(it))
        R2 = int(next(it)); W2 = int(next(it)); C2 = int(next(it))
        a_win = (R1 > R2) + (W1 > W2) + (C1 > C2)
        b_win = 3 - a_win
        out_lines.append('A' if a_win > b_win else 'B')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
